title: Fluffy Feta Greek Cheese Tiropita
description: A classic Greek tiropita recipe featuring fluffy feta cheese, sweet Castelvetrano olives, and a glassy jelly glaze, making it a perfect low-carb friendly dinner option.
instructions: Thaw the phyllo dough according to package instructions.
Combine feta cheese, egg, Greek yogurt, honey, fresh dill, and lemon zest in a bowl. Mix well.
Cut the phyllo dough into 4 equal pieces and spread a small amount of feta mixture in the center of each piece.
Fold the phyllo dough over the feta mixture and press the edges to seal. Repeat with the remaining phyllo dough and feta mixture.
Brush the tiropita with a little bit of olive oil and bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the tiropita is baking, prepare the glaze by heating the honey in a saucepan over low heat until runny.
Once the tiropita is done, brush the glaze over the top and serve immediately.
To make the jelly glaze, combine 1 cup water with 1 cup granulated sugar in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened into a jelly-like consistency.
Remove the jelly glaze from heat and stir in 1 tsp (5g) of lemon zest.
To assemble the tiropita, place a few Castelvetrano olives on top of the feta mixture and drizzle with a little bit of jelly glaze.
